ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION 503, DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. HEARING HELD AND TENTATIVE RULING ADOPTED AS MODIFIED: DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED. DEFENDANT FAILED TO SUSTAIN ITS BURDEN OF DEMONSTRATING THAT PLAINTIFF DOES NOT POSSESS AND CANNOT REASONABLY OBTAIN EVIDENCE THAT HE WAS EXPOSED TO ASBESTOS-CONTAINING PRODUCTS OR MATERIALS ATTRIBUTABLE TO DEFENDANT. PLAINTIFF'S DEPOSITION TESTIMONY AND RESPONSES TO DEFENDANT'S SPECIAL INTERROGATORIES PRECLUDE DEFENDANT FROM SUCCESSFULLY INVOKING THE FACTUALLY DEVOID PRONG OF AGUILAR. DEFENDANT'S ALTERNATE MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION OF PLAINTIFF'S STRICT LIABILITY CAUSE OF ACTION WILL BE CONTINUED PURSUANT TO PLAINTIFF'S REQUEST FOR A CONTINUANCE PURSUANT TO C.C.P. SECTION 437C(H), WHICH THE COURT GRANTS. THE HEARING IS CONTINUED TO OCTOBER 17, 2012 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPARTMENT 503, SUPPLEMENTAL OPPOSITION DUE ON 10/11 BY 4P.M. James P. Nevin of BRAYTON & PURCELL LLP (415-898-1555), appearing for plaintiff / opposing party John Gossett

RULING:
DEFENDANT GOLDEN GATE DRYWALL, INC.'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT

Hearing held and tentative ruling adopted as modified: Defendant Golden Gate Drywall, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of demonstrating that plaintiff does not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that he was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Plaintiff's deposition testimony and responses to defendant's special interrogatories preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Defendant's alternate motion for summary adjudication of plaintiff's strict liability cause of action will be continued pursuant to plaintiff's request for a continuance pursuant to C.C.P. section 437c(h), which the court grants. The hearing is continued to October 17, 2012 at 9:30 a.m. in Department 503, supplemental opposition due on 10/11 by 4p.m. and supplemental reply due on 10/15 by 4p.m. Order signed in open court. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Reporter: Kim Richardson of AIKEN WELCH, CSR # 5915
(503/TLJ)
